channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: El coste emocional del liderazgo: mayor bienestar pero más estrés y soledad
item.description: Sin embargo, también son quienes experimentan con más frecuencia emociones negativas como el estrés, la tristeza, la ira o la soledad. Los líderes tienen una valoración más positiva de sus vidas y muestran mayores niveles de compromiso con su trabajo que el resto de los empleados.

item.title: BHSI ficha a César Morales Eguía para impulsar el negocio en el norte
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Specialty Insurance (BHSI) ha confirmado lo adelantado hace unos días por el BDS: la apertura de una nueva oficina en Bilbao y la incorporación de César Morales Eguía como suscriptor de Responsabilidad Civil, con el objetivo de reforzar su presencia comercial en el norte de España.

item.title: «No hay nada más terapéutico que sentir que alguien entiende por lo que estás pasando»
item.description: Sara Sorribes se ha convertido en uno de los referentes más visibles de la importancia de cuidar la salud mental en el deporte de élite.

item.title: Scouring Strait of Hormuz for Mines Could Take Weeks, Security Firms Say
item.description: Ensuring the Strait of Hormuz is safe from mines could delay a return to normal shipping traffic by weeks following a deal to reopen the waterway, shipping and maritime security sources say. The operation by conventional minesweepers and state-of-the-art underwater drones could continue for 40 to 50 days before many insurance, shipping or oil companies are confident enough to sail through, according to assessments from five Western maritime security sources. “The threat of mines in the area remains a concern immediately as well as further down the line and mine-free routes need to be established.” It is unclear how many mines Iran may have laid in the strait, which handled 20% of the world’s daily supply of oil and liquefied natural gas before the war.

item.title: Nonprofit Hospital Entitled to Liability Cap But Not Immunity: NJ High Court
item.description: The CIA that provides nonprofit entities immunity from negligence claims if they are “organized exclusively for religious, charitable or educational purposes,” also sets forth an exception to immunity for nonprofit corporations organized “exclusively for hospital purposes.” In those cases, the law limits their liability “to an amount not exceeding $250,000, together with interest and costs of suit, as the result of any one accident.” The high court said there is no dispute that NCHC is not organized exclusively for religious purposes. (NCHC) is instead entitled only to a $250,000 cap on damages as provided by the charitable immunity law because it is “organized exclusively for hospital purposes,” the high court found in reversing lower courts on the immunity issue. A unanimous New Jersey Supreme Court ruled on June 10 that a federally qualified nonprofit health center that provides healthcare to underserved populations is not entitled to immunity from a patient’s negligence suit under the state’s Charitable Immunity Act (CIA) that shields entities “organized exclusively for religious, charitable or educational purposes.” Rather, Newark Community Health Centers, Inc.

item.title: The AI Medical Summary Explained: What They Are, How They Work, and Why They Matter in Claims
item.description: The difference isn’t luck or experience; it’s an AI medical summary that transforms how insurance carriers , legal teams , and TPAs cut through the noise to get to what actually matters. Medical report summary AI doesn’t just pull random sentences; it understands medical terminology, recognizes patterns across different document types, and presents information in formats that claims professionals can immediately use, such as AI medical chronologies , key facts in an AI medical summaries , or detailed medical record summaries per record. What sets modern medical record summary tools apart is the Human-in-the-Loop (HITL) model.

item.title: Cyber insurance limit adequacy under the microscope across Asia as average ransomware event cost tops US$5m
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: India sells up to 5% of GIC Re via share placing
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Descartes Underwriting gains Lloyd’s coverholder status via new Oak Global partnership
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Later life lending: building the fourth retirement pillar
item.description: Speaker: Emad Aladhal, director of retail banking Event: Later Life Lending Summit Delivered: 16 June 2026 Note: This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version Reading time: 10 minutes In the years ahead, housing wealth will become an increasing part of how many people provide for their retirement. Speech by Emad Aladhal, director of retail banking at the Later Life Lending Summit. None brought up later life lending, or how they could use their home to help.

item.title: Descartes Underwriting teams with OAK Global to deliver parametric solutions via Lloyd’s
item.description: Descartes Underwriting, the parametric risk transfer specialist, and OAK Global, the specialist Lloyd’s underwriting company, has announced Descartes’ approval as a Coverholder at Lloyd’s, which will see the firm underwrite parametric policies through OAK Global’s Syndicate 2843, bringing new risks to the London market. As a Lloyd’s Coverholder, Descartes will now gain direct access to the Lloyd’s underwriting capacity, enabling its growth into new strategic markets, particularly the High-Net-Worth individual segment in the United States, which seeks innovative solutions much like parametric, for insuring against natural disaster events. “This designation represents a major achievement for Descartes, as it demonstrates the confidence of OAK Global and Lloyd’s as a whole in our underwriting expertise, governance framework, and operational capabilities,” commented Tanguy Touffut, Co-founder and CEO of Descartes Underwriting.

item.title: Guy Carpenter hires Tregidga from Compre, for legacy and sidecar structured solutions focus
item.description: Tregidga joins Guy Carpenter in London and the company said she will “lead on legacy deals and sidecar activity, strengthening our capabilities in two areas that continue to grow in strategic importance.” The reinsurance broker further stated, “With a background that combines legal training and extensive transaction experience, Connie brings additional depth to our structured solutions offering as we support clients through changing market dynamics.” Tregidga joins Guy Carpenter from legacy, run-off and specialty reinsurance group Compre where she held the role of Group M&A Director. Guy Carpenter, the global reinsurance broking firm, has announced the hire of Connie Tregidga as a Managing Director in its Global Structured Solutions team, with legacy deals and reinsurance sidecars a focus for her new role. Her expertise in retrospective and legacy solutions, while having worked through a major casualty reinsurance sidecar deal from the perspective of an investor participant and provider of exit solutions capabilities will position Tregidga well to assist Guy Carpenter clients in accessing new and alternative capital sources, in structured formats.

item.title: CRC appoints Goldstein to lead proprietary capacity, alternative capital partnerships division
item.description: CRC Group, a wholesale insurance services and distribution specialist, has launched a new Risk Solutions Division to advance its focus on developing proprietary capacity, capital partnerships and alternative risk solutions, including its reinsurance sidecars, appointing Bill Goldstein to lead the division. In his new position leading the capital partnerships focused division, Goldstein will work to develop strategic capacity, including proprietary structures such as sidecars. CRC Group has now launched its newly created Risk Solutions Division to bring initiatives like this under one roof, naming Bill Goldstein as Head of Strategic Partnerships, Capacity and Capital Solutions.

item.title: Westfield Specialty launches UK & Europe liability portfolio, led by Keith Knight
item.description: Westfield Specialty International, a specialty insurance provider, has expanded its casualty offering with the launch of a new UK and Europe liability portfolio. Ross MacDonald, Divisional Director, Casualty and Portfolio, Westfield Specialty International, commented, “At Westfield, we have the appetite and capacity to take on new risks to grow our book, and the launch of the UK and Europe liability portfolio is a key step in that strategy. The UK and Europe liability portfolio will underwrite a broad range of liability risks, supporting clients operating across multiple jurisdictions and legal environments.

item.title: Willis report finds cyber insurance covers majority of data breach and first-party losses
item.description: In its latest report, Cyber claims in focus – getting value from cyber insurance, Willis found that insurance covers more than 95% of average data breach losses and 90% of average first-party losses. The report also highlighted the growing role of third parties in cyber losses, with external organisations responsible for nearly 50% of data breach losses and 29% of first-party losses. According to Willis, data breaches remain the most frequently reported cyber insurance claims, with malicious breaches accounting for the majority of incidents.

item.title: McKinsey executive Pradip Patiath joins AXIS Board of Directors
item.description: AXIS Capital Holdings Limited has appointed Pradip Patiath, presently a Senior Partner at McKinsey & Company and a global leader in the firm’s Financial Services and Technology division, to its Board of Directors. Effective June 15, 2026, Patiath will join AXIS’s Board, serving on its Risk and Human Capital and Compensation Committees. Marston Becker, Chair of the Board at AXIS, commented, “Pradip is recognised as one of the foremost thinkers and practitioners in digital strategy in the financial services sector.

item.title: Ukraine Aims to Align Banks, Insurers With EU Rules by 2028, Central Banker Says
item.description: Ukraine is accelerating reforms to align its banking and insurance sectors with EU standards by 2028 despite wartime challenges, to buttress investor confidence and deepen economic integration with Europe, its central bank governor said. Banks are well capitalized, and the central bank is actively seeking to implement reforms required for EU integration, Pyshnyi said. Pyshnyi said the central bank is implementing an overhaul of Ukraine’s insurance sector to make it more transparent, stable, and attractive to investors.

item.title: US-Iran Deal Promises End to War but How it Will Work Remains Unclear
item.description: Doubts swirled around the U.S.-Iran interim deal to end the war in the Middle East as shippers said it could take weeks for confidence to return after any reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and fundamental questions remained unanswered. To be sure, the deal is the most significant step yet to resolve the conflict, which has killed at least 7,000 people, mostly in Iran and Lebanon, and upended global energy markets. and Iran, although details have yet to be made public and both countries said a permanent truce was yet to be negotiated.

item.title: Lockton names Stéphane Lespérance Canada CEO
item.description: Lockton announced the appointment of Stéphane Lespérance as CEO of its Canadian business, marking a significant step in the broker’s efforts to expand its footprint in Canada. Lespérance joins Lockton after a 24-year career at Aon Canada, where he served as president since 2019 and was named president and CEO in 2025. He brings the experience, credibility, and strategic vision required to deliver a premier advisory experience for clients across Canada.” – Ron Lockton, Chairman and CEO, Lockton.

item.title: Pints AI raises $5.6 million for auditable AI
item.description: Pints AI , a Singapore-based provider of AI systems for regulated financial institutions, raised $5.6 million in a Pre-Series A funding round led by Tin Men Capital, with SBI Ven Capital serving as co-lead investor. Founded in 2021, its Autothought platform is used by 12 financial institutions across four countries, generating more than $10 million in collective savings. According to Pints AI, the platform connects to an institution’s core systems, routes tasks to different AI models—including proprietary small language models, frontier models, and open-source models—and creates an audit trail for every decision.

item.title: Corporate travel insurers brace for more Middle East war-related claims as Iran conflict continues
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Markel accelerates Asia push in bid to rebalance global book, but ‘not before we are ready’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: BMO launches health and dental insurance offering powered by GreenShield
item.description: BMO Insurance has launched a new health and dental insurance offering for Canadians through its digital brokerage arm, BMO Insurance Solutions Inc. The new product, BMO Health and Dental Insurance, is underwritten by Green Shield Canada Insurance and combines traditional health and dental benefits with digital health services. GreenShield, Canada’s largest non-profit health and benefits organization, operates an integrated payer-provider model that combines insurance administration with healthcare services including telemedicine, mental health support, pharmacy services, and chronic disease management.

item.title: Zurich launches AI-powered coaching platform for dealership F&I managers
item.description: Zurich North America has launched Zurich Advisor IQ, an AI-powered training and coaching platform to help automotive finance and insurance (F&I) managers improve performance using insights derived from real customer interactions. Zurich Advisor IQ brings AI-powered coaching directly into the flow of real deals, giving F&I teams the confidence to perform at a higher level in every interaction.” – Head of Sales for Zurich Direct Markets, Dante Pileri. According to Zurich, Advisor IQ can help dealerships standardize F&I execution across locations, identify behavioral trends affecting results, accelerate onboarding for new managers, and provide visibility into metrics such as profit per vehicle (PVR), products per deal (PPD), and product utilization rates.

item.title: Willis launches builder’s risk facility for wood frame construction projects
item.description: The post Willis launches builder’s risk facility for wood frame construction projects appeared first on Coverager - Insurance news and insights . Willis launched the Wood Frame Follow Facility Builder’s Risk Program, a new insurance facility designed for ground-up wood frame construction projects across the United States. The program provides follow-form builder’s risk capacity that aligns with lead market coverage, targeting multifamily residential developments, mixed-use projects, and other wood frame construction risks.

item.title: Hippo Promotes Laura Boettcher to COO
item.description: (San Jose, Calif.) has promoted Laura Boettcher, ARe, to COO of Hippo Holdings, the parent company of Hippo Insurance. Boettcher will lead operations across the enterprise after previously serving as COO of Hippo Insurance. “Laura has been an invaluable part of Hippo’s leadership team for nearly five years, and she has earned the trust of our executive council and board of directors,” comments Rick McCathron, President and CEO, Hippo Holdings.

item.title: Plymouth Rock Promotes Michelle Trindade to COO
item.description: (Image source: Plymouth Rock Assurance website.) Plymouth Rock Assurance (Woodbridge, N.J.) has promoted Michelle Trindade to COO, Plymouth Rock Management Company of New Jersey, and Nilu Tabrizi to VP, Customer Service, Plymouth Rock Management Company of New Jersey. Michelle is a proven leader who brings a unique combination of customer focus, business acumen, and execution excellence, making her the right person to help lead the next chapter of growth for our Direct Group.” Trindade joined Plymouth Rock in 2025 and most recently served as VP of Marketing and Customer Service for the company’s Direct Group. Focus on Growth, CX, Operations and Tech Transformation Michelle Trindade to COO, Direct Group, Plymouth Rock Management Company of New Jersey.

item.title: Liberty Mutual and Satellite Firm ICEYE Launch Parametric Wildfire Insurance
item.description: Liberty Mutual Insurance and space intelligence firm ICEYE have partnered to launch a parametric wildfire insurance coverage that works with satellites. Liberty Mutual’s parametric wildfire coverage offers scalable protection that complements traditional insurance while ICEYE’s global constellation of Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR) satellites provides continuous monitoring of wildfire-affected areas. “By pushing the boundaries of technology, we are delivering a new generation of parametric wildfire coverage that is faster, more responsive, and fully data driven,” stated Jean-Christophe Garaix, head of Parametrics & Agriculture at Liberty, in the launch announcement.

item.title: The Case for a Personal Digital Bodyguard
item.description: By shifting from a reactive cyber policy to a proactive, preventive risk-management offering, insurance brokers and underwriters can provide clients with a “digital bodyguard” to protect them while safeguarding their bottom line. As cybercrime hits $21 billion, personal cyber insurance must pivot from reactive coverage to proactive protection. According to its 2025 report, cybercrime costs reached a record $20.8 billion in losses last year, sounding the alarm bells on the critical need for personal cyber insurance policies that offer proactive risk management.

item.title: Merit Financial partners with Prizm
item.description: Wealth management firm Merit Financial Advisors announced a partnership with insurance broker Prizm Insurance. Merit has built an exceptional advisory business centered around comprehensive planning and client experience, and we’re excited to partner with their team to make insurance easier, more transparent, and more integrated into the financial planning process.” – Dave Olchowka, CEO and Co-Founder of Prizm. Prizm’s modern approach to insurance aligns closely with our commitment to comprehensive planning and helps ensure our clients’ insurance coverage keeps pace with their evolving financial lives.” – Alex Hansen, Chief Advisor Success Officer at Merit Financial.

item.title: LenderDock announces expanded partnership with Idaho Farm Bureau
item.description: LenderDock announced an expanded partnership with Idaho Farm Bureau Insurance, building on the insurer’s existing use of the company’s NOTiFi solution. As part of the expanded relationship, Idaho Farm Bureau will implement LenderDock’s VERiFi and LIENSure platforms. These tools allow us to streamline processes, improve accuracy, and deliver faster, more efficient service across the board.” – Idaho Farm Bureau Director of Marketing & Customer Experience Dana May.

item.title: Evaluación de solvencia: la clave para una concesión de crédito responsable
item.description: La transparencia, la evaluación de solvencia y la información clara sobre costes son piezas clave para reducir el riesgo de sobreendeudamiento y reforzar la protección del cliente en la contratación de crédito. El crédito al consumo está bajo una vigilancia regulatoria más intensa.

item.title: Sixfold Launches AI Underwriter
item.description: (Image source: Sixfold homepage.) Sixfold (New York) has launched AI Underwriter, an underwriting agent for property/casualty insurers that assesses submissions, checks missing information, evaluates appetite and portfolio fit, and recommends next actions for underwriters. “What does a great underwriter actually need the moment they receive a submission?” comments Alex Schmelkin, Founder and CEO, Sixfold. Instead, our users told us that what they need is to understand the risk in the full context of their current book, the broker, and the guidelines, and to be presented with a clear next step.” Carrier-Specific Underwriting Context Sixfold says AI Underwriter is built on an underwriting model trained on professional standards, underwriter reasoning patterns and a curated library across lines and industries.

item.title: Origami Adds Insurance Program Management Tools
item.description: (Image source: Origami Risk website.) Origami Risk (Chicago) has introduced Insurance Program Management capabilities within its Risk Management Information System (RMIS) solution. The release describes insurance program management as an area where data is often spread across PDFs, spreadsheets, emails and broker systems. “Risk teams are skilled at managing complex insurance programs,” comments Peter Richard, Director of Product Management, Origami Risk.

item.title: Homeowner Claims Support Platform Tugboat Closes $3M Seed Round
item.description: Tugboat, a consumer insurance platform that helps homeowners with property damage and insurance claims, announced an oversubscribed $3 million in seed funding to scale its platform and offer expanded claim support for homeowners. The Simi Valley-based company said the funding will support: building a fully integrated platform with API connections to major property management systems; accelerating embedded business-to-business distribution through partnerships with multifamily property owners, lenders, and restoration networks; and sustaining Tugboat’s free disaster-response program, which activates during designated disaster events with no membership required. The round was led by ResilienceVC with participation from South Dakota First, gener8tor, Sure Ventures, Acumen America and other investors.

item.title: U.S. High Court Rebuffs Challenge to New York Law Allowing Suits Against Gun Industry
item.description: Supreme Court turned away on Monday a gun industry challenge to a New York law that permits lawsuits against gun makers, wholesalers and dealers for endangering people’s safety through sales of firearms and ammunition. The justices declined to hear an appeal by an industry trade group, the National Shooting Sports Foundation, of a lower court’s ruling upholding the law, which New York calls a public nuisance statute. The New York law, signed by former Democratic Governor Andrew Cuomo in 2021, requires the gun industry to use reasonable safeguards to protect against gun trafficking, theft, and the use of “straw purchasers” who buy firearms for someone else.

item.title: Scientists Concerned El Niño Will Be Big, Bad and Costly With Heat, Floods, Droughts, Fires
item.description: Meteorologists forecast it will rival — or exceed — a record El Niño that began in 1997 and helped trigger billions of dollars in damage from heat waves, floods, droughts, tornadoes, and wildfires. Experts said the El Niño, a natural warming cycle, should further heat a globe already warming from fossil fuel pollution and will likely turbocharge extreme weather across the planet. NOAA’s announcement said there’s a 63% chance that the El Niño will get so intense this late fall and early winter that it “would rank among the largest El Niño events in the historical record going back to 1950.” The warm, deep waters of an El Niño affect weather patterns by bringing “a lot of extra heat to the surface, fueling a lot of extreme events for a lot of places around the world,” said Clark University climate scientist Abby Frazier.

item.title: Major Gap Between Identity Security Confidence and Reality: Study
item.description: A new report uncovered a significant disconnect between enterprise confidence in identity security and operational reality, according to identity technology providers FIDO Alliance and HID. The report, “The State of Physical and Digital Identity in the Enterprise,” also found that 70% experienced at least one identity-related security incident overall. “Identity security is no longer just an authentication challenge; it is an enterprise governance challenge.

item.title: Augment Risk strengthens ILS team with Brad Livingston hire, targets private ILS expansion
item.description: Augment Risk, the risk capital and reinsurance solutions broking firm, has hired well-known insurance-linked securities market executive Brad Livingston, who joins the firm to strengthen its ILS team and in particular is expected to help grow the private ILS offering in property risk, given his collateralized reinsurance expertise. Livingston joins Augment Risk as a partner within its ILS team, effective immediately. At Augment Risk, Livingston will be tasked with expanding the ILS offering at the broker, in particular around private ILS and collateralized reinsurance.

item.title: Stone Point raises $610.5m in first close of new Insurance Solutions Fund
item.description: Stone Point Capital LLC, the private equity and credit investment firm with an insurance and reinsurance specialism, has successfully raised $610.5 million in a first close for a new strategy, named the Stone Point Insurance Solutions Fund. In the case of this new strategy, these first close commitments totalling $610.5 million have been made to a Cayman Islands structure named the Stone Point Insurance Solutions Fund, L.P. Given the lack of information, we cannot confirm that the above is the precise mandate for deployment of this new Stone Point Insurance Solutions Fund.

item.title: Aon’s White Rock issues $14.85m Raptor Re 2026-1 private cat bond
item.description: A new private catastrophe bond has come to market via an Aon operated White Rock Insurance entity that we believe to be domiciled in Guernsey, with a $14.85 million tranche of Raptor Re 2026-1 notes privately placed with qualified investors. The White Rock Insurance platform is managed by Aon Insurance Managers, so that unit will have played a role in getting this Raptor Re 2026-1 private cat bond, or cat bond lite, to market. Insurance and reinsurance broking group Aon has been utilising its range of White Rock entities for the structuring and issuance of private catastrophe bond and collateralized reinsurance arrangements for around two decades.

item.title: Data centre boom opens ‘considerable opportunity’ for US P&C insurers: AM Best
item.description: A new AM Best report has suggested that the growing proliferation of data centre projects presents a “considerable opportunity” for the U.S. Similar dynamics were highlighted in a recent report from S&P Global on hyperscale data centre campuses , which found construction-phase total insured values reaching $20bn–$50bn as markets adjust to a step-change in asset scale, pointing to a growing but increasingly selective opportunity for re/insurers. David Blades, associate director, Industry Research and Analytics, AM Best, commented, “As data centre development and construction spreads, the required insurance coverage is evolving, as it is currently beyond what the traditional property/casualty industry has previously experienced.” The rating agency’s report noted that “hyperscale” data centres are currently attracting the most attention and scrutiny due to their scale, rapid expansion, and significant effects on local communities.

item.title: Hippo Holdings names Laura Boettcher as Chief Operating Officer for group-wide operations
item.description: Hippo Holdings Inc., the US-based parent company of Hippo Insurance, has announced the promotion of Laura Boettcher, ARe, to Chief Operating Officer. Rick McCathron, President and Chief Executive Officer of Hippo Holdings, commented on the appointment, saying, “Laura has been an invaluable part of Hippo’s leadership team for nearly five years, and she has earned the trust of our executive council and board of directors,” said Rick McCathron, President and CEO of Hippo Holdings. She had been serving as Chief Operating Officer of Hippo Insurance since February 2024, where the company says she was responsible for core operational activity while balancing service delivery and business requirements.

item.title: Juan Naveda to lead Dynamic Re as CEO, London
item.description: Dynamic Reinsurance, a firm specialising in tailored reinsurance risk transfer solutions, has appointed Juan Naveda as Chief Executive Officer (CEO), London. Naveda has over three decades of insurance and reinsurance industry experience, and brings extensive expertise and strong relationships across the London market. The reinsurer, in a statement, commented on the appointment, “Juan’s technical expertise, market insight, and leadership will be instrumental as we continue to strengthen our capabilities and advance Dynamic Reinsurance’s international growth strategy.

item.title: Florida Tries to Block Rescue Pets from New Mexico and Texas as Screwworm Spreads
item.description: The Florida Department of Agriculture and Consumer Services Pets has barred pets rescued from shelters in Texas and New Mexico in a beefed-up effort to stop the spread of the New World Screwworm pest that infests livestock. Related: Screwworm Parasite Spreads Beyond Texas Texas Governor Vows Sped Up Construction of Screwworm Fly Breeding Facility The emergency rule, seen here , expands restrictions on animal imports from affected areas, prohibits the importation of rescue and shelter dogs and cats from states with confirmed screwworm detections, and revises the definition of infested zones to any county with a confirmed screwworm detection and its surrounding counties, the statement noted.

item.title: Missouri Sues Baby Monitor Manufacturer for Alleged Links to Chinese Military
item.description: The lawsuit alleges Lorex has concealed material ties to the Chinese military via its manufacturer, Dahua, putting families at risk. Department of Defense designating Dahua as a Chinese Military Company that threatens national security, according to the Missouri Attorney General’s Office. Missouri Attorney General Catherine Hanaway has filed a lawsuit against Lorex Corporation, a major seller of home cameras and baby monitors.

item.title: Allianz emerges as frontrunner to acquire HSBC Life Singapore in potential US$2bn deal
item.description: The post Allianz emerges as frontrunner to acquire HSBC Life Singapore in potential US$2bn deal appeared first on Insurtech Insights . German insurance giant Allianz is reportedly leading the race to acquire HSBC Life Singapore as HSBC Holdings explores strategic options for its insurance business in the city-state, according to reports citing sources familiar with the matter. For Allianz, an acquisition of HSBC Life Singapore would represent another major opportunity to strengthen its position in the Singapore market following an unsuccessful attempt to acquire a majority stake in Income Insurance in 2024.

item.title: Goosehead names Eben Hewitt as CTO
item.description: Goosehead Insurance announced the appointment of Eben Hewitt as Chief Technology Officer. Hewitt joins Goosehead from Hyatt Corporation, where he served as Global Chief Information Officer, overseeing engineering, data and AI, infrastructure, cybersecurity, technology operations, and enterprise applications. As CTO, Hewitt will lead Goosehead’s technology strategy, including the expansion of AI-powered capabilities, data and analytics platforms, and the continued evolution of Digital Agent 2.0.

item.title: Simplicity acquires August H. Velten & Associates
item.description: As part of the transaction, founder August Velten will become a Simplicity Partner, and the registered investment advisor is expected to rebrand as Simplicity Wealth Advisors: Melbourne. Simplicity Group Holdings has acquired August H. Velten & Associates, an independent retail wealth management and annuity producer based in Melbourne, Florida.

item.title: GetCovered acquires Revyse
item.description: GetCovered, a risk and compliance platform for multifamily housing operators, announced the acquisition of Revyse, a full-service vendor compliance and contract management company. The Revyse platform provides a single system to manage bids, contracts, credentials, and spend. The acquisition will complement GetCovered’s existing vendor risk and compliance capabilities with enhanced tools for contract management, spend intelligence, and AI-powered vendor compliance automation.
